mentions.note: Strong earnings spurring first positive recommendation in 17+ years; Cramer hated it since 1847, now says time to look forward, not backward.
Semiconductor capital equipment beneficiary of fab buildout; CEO Kerry Diggerson securing long-term contracts ensuring earnings bumps; up 131% YTD.
Data center compute architecture beneficiary; up 283% YTD; part of endless price target raise cycle on data center growth.
Bank leadership on M&A/IPO bonanza ($1.2T YTD), deregulation tailwinds, and higher rates; still trading at low multiples with significant upside.
Cramer admits he was 'not bullish enough'; reported GAAP profitability Q1 and guided full-year profitability, but prefers UNH and CBS as superior healthcare plays.
GAAP profitable Q1 with full-year guidance, but Cramer focused on UnitedHealth and Cigna instead; acknowledges wasn't bullish enough earlier.
Bank rally participant; benefiting from tame delinquencies and strong consumer demand; higher rate environment helps credit card economics.
CEO George Kurts managing long-cycle sales; Q1 beat despite selloff; second half expected strong due to MYTHOs adoption driving near-term upside.
Sold off after Q1 but mythos scare will drive Q2+ adoption as long-cycle sales materialize; second half outlook strong.
Data center compute supplier benefiting from AI infrastructure buildout; up 233% YTD; analyst price target leapfrog continuing.

Ford Energy battery storage subsidiary transforms EV losses into 20+ GW annual capacity data center power supply by late 2027; first positive recommendation in years now justified by lower oil/rate environment; trades 8x earnings, 4.3% dividend.
Cramer likes company fundamentally but avoiding due to AI disruption risk; unwilling to hold 'crosshairs' names vulnerable to AI replacement.
Cramer likes company fundamentals but won't recommend due to AI disruption risk to credit models; too painful if AI replaces traditional credit scoring.
Investment banking 'crushing it' on M&A advisory, IPO issuance, and bond underwriting; massive profit opportunity from $1.2T M&A and bond issuance pipeline.
Tate & Lyle acquisition elevates to 55% texture + health solutions revenue; 65 plants, 800 scientists globally; insurgent brands need this innovation; will be most important company in its industry.
CPU demand explosion from agentic AI as GPU-to-CPU ratio shifts from 8:1 toward 1:1; CEO Lip Bu Tan fixing foundry business; potential Apple and Musk orders on 18A node; own it, don't trade it.
Bank rally has staying power on moderately higher rates (NIM expansion), strong consumer (May retail +6.9% YoY), tame delinquencies; still cheap; 50%+ upside before reasonably priced.
Semiconductor equipment supplier with commodity chip shortages driving demand; up 96% YTD.
Semiconductor equipment shortage—up 96% YTD; benefiting from bottleneck in capital equipment needed to make commodity semis.
Semiconductor fab equipment play; shortage in capital equipment creating demand; up 190% YTD.
Up stock—buy every dip with conviction; Cramer willing to pull the trigger every time it declines; best hotel company in the space.
Data center semiconductor and component supplier; up 241% YTD; part of AI infrastructure buildout.
Investment banking powerhouse 'crushing it' on M&A and capital markets; massive profit opportunity from IPO wave and M&A advisory.
DRAM shortage driving 266% gain YTD; price target raises continue; can't make chips fast enough; part of endless shortage play.
Hyperscaler raising $25B in debt despite fortress balance sheet; benefiting from massive AI capex cycle and hyperscaler competition driving continued chip demand.
Up 93%, revenue +21%, massive debt load alongside CoreWeave; fine for leverage play but highly leveraged position.
Heavy debt load but 'fine' as a spring-load play on cloud/AI growth; beaten-down valuation offers leverage for those seeking it.
Ana Botín leading brilliant acquisition of Webster Financial; exemplary M&A play under deregulation; Cramer points to as model for other large banks with AI tech to roll up rivals.

Digital transformation and autonomous drilling unlocking secular growth in oil services; 20-year Nvidia partnership enabling LLM tuning and high-performance computing; energy security tailwind accelerating adoption.
Memory and storage shortage play up 725% YTD—phenomenal gains from DRAM shortages and critical chip supply constraints.
Storage shortage beneficiary, up 287% YTD; continuous price increases on constrained supply; data center demand insatiable.
Cramer admits wrong on consolidation thesis; expected Stryker to acquire/consolidate (e.g., Zimmer Biomet); didn't materialize; stock underperformed; 'real tough ride'.
Expected consolidation with Zimmer Biomet never materialized; disappointing performer; won't sell but thesis broken.
First positive recommendation in 17+ years after strong earnings; Cramer likes ADM and Tyson as a pair; companies transformed fundamentally.
Oversold—down 80 points from highs, rebounded to 60 down; Cramer blesses recovery; suggests 25% position sizing on falling-knife setup.
Down 80 points from high then bounced 60; 'too low' now; blessed to buy but only size 25% position due to volatility; falling knife but cheap.
Memory and storage shortage play, up 313% YTD; price target raises ongoing; Apple citing memory constraints to justify price hikes.
Bank rally beneficiary on rate environment, strong consumer, deregulation; cheap valuation leaves room for 50%+ rally before reasonably priced.
